title: Sequential Characterization of Discontinuity
statement: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$, let $f:A \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $c \in A$. The function $f$ is discontinuous at $c$ if and only if there exists a sequence $(x_n)_{n\in\mathbb{N}} \subseteq A$ such that $x_n \to c$ while $f(x_n) \not\to f(c)$.

current_dependencies.title: Continuity at a Point --- Sequential Form
